headline: Disney Facial Recognition Lawsuit Puts Privacy And Valuation In Focus
summary: Disney is facing a $5 million lawsuit over the use of facial recognition technology at Disneyland and Disney California Adventure Park in California. The complaint focuses on how biometric data, including information on children, is collected, disclosed, stored, and whether guests can opt out. The case raises fresh questions about privacy practices and regulatory compliance tied to Disney's theme park operations. Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) is under legal pressure at the same time its stock...
